mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present values and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of real GDP growth and commercial/industrial loan balances indicates synchronized demand and credit tightening, which compresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression of 20-day SPX returns, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 5% in hit rate.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained industrial production expansion above 105, elevated capacity utilization translates into higher corporate operating leverage, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings catch up to output.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are flat or negative when INDPRO exceeds 105 for two consecutive months, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.2 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in housing starts reflects rising consumer confidence and forward-looking demand, which positively impacts financials, materials, and consumer discretionary sectors, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between HOUST month-over-month changes and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.05, or the signal generates losses in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.25 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to pressure high-multiple growth stocks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%, or the signal's directional accuracy fails to exceed 55% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in unemployment and decline in nonfarm payrolls confirms a broad-based labor market cooling, which historically precedes earnings recessions and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal's hit rate does not exceed 65% over a 5-year out-of-sample period, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation surprises.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.0 percent, positive revisions to real GDP growth are interpreted by markets as persistent demand-pull pressures rather than earnings tailwinds, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of high-inflation GDP growth episodes, or the conditional Sharpe ratio exceeds 0.5 over a 3-year rolling window.
